raw_consumer_statement: In XX/XX/year>, I contracted with a moving company for an interstate move after they repeatedly assured me that pickup would occur on XX/XX/XXXX and that all work would be performed by their own employees using company-branded trucks, with no subcontractors involved. Based on these promises, I paid a {$1500.00} deposit on XX/XX/year>. The company failed to give the required XXXX pickup notice and repeatedly shifted the pickup window on XX/XX/XXXX and XXXX. Pickup ultimately did not occur until XX/XX/XXXX. When the crew arrived, the entire job was performed by an undisclosed subcontractor, XXXX XXXX, contrary to the promises made at booking. That same day, I was charged {$1300.00} by the subcontractor for pickup. At pickup, they attempted to impose a fabricated {$2700.00} upcharge claiming XXXX extra cubic feet, despite having completed both audio and video inventories and providing a written estimate ; they waived this charge only after pressuring me to verbally agree not to post a negative review. No revised written estimate was ever issued as required by federal rules. Over the two days of delays, I spent about 158 minutes on calls with representatives who gave contradictory information, dismissed concerns, made unprofessional remarks, and at times threatened to end calls. Delivery occurred later the same day, and one item arrived damaged. When I filed a damage claim, the company denied it and then refused to provide the federally required arbitration instructions despite multiple written requests, violating 49 CFR 375.211. I disputed both charges with my credit card issuerthe {$1500.00} deposit obtained through misrepresentation and the {$1300.00} subcontractor charge obtained through delays, coercion, and undisclosed subcontractingbut the issuer denied both disputes without addressing the evidence. I also filed a certified demand letter and complaints with FMCSA and the XXXX, but the company continued refusing to remedy the misrepresentations, delays, coercion, and regulatory violations. Both charges were obtained through deceptive practices and failure to perform the services as promised.
